title_en: 5. Would you like to meet him outside the institution? (cont.)
title_id: 5. Maukah Kamu Menemuinya di Luar Lembaga? (lanjutan)
content_en: Meaning, if the advice is given in an appropriate manner with tenderness from the beginning, the end result is often the same. But if it is given harshly and callously, the end result will be similar. When we are advising people, we are actually dealing with their hearts and not their bodies. This is why, children often take their mother's advice but not the father's, or vice versa and students tend to accept one teacher's advice but not another's. The very ﬁrst skill one should use while giving advice is not to do it excessively and pick on every fault, big or small, so that others do not feel that you are constantly watching their every move. Otherwise, they would see you as very cumbersome. The leader of a people is not the one who is foolish The leader of a people is the one who pretends to be foolish If you can present your advice as a suggestion instead, then you should do so. For example: your wife brings you dinner and you know that she has tired herself preparing this, and it happens to be very salty. Do not say, "Oh no! What kind of meal is this? I seek refuge in Allah! It seems you emptied out the whole packet of salt in this!" Rather, you should say, "If you were to have decreased the amount of salt, it would have tasted even better." Similarly, if you notice your son wearing dirty clothes, then advise him as if you are making a suggestion, because people do not like to be ordered around. Say to him, "How nice would it be if you were to wear better clothes?" If a student comes late to school, say to him, "How nice would it be for you not to be late again?" This approach is far superior. Use this approach all the time, "How about if you do this? I suggest you do that…" This approach is much better than for you to say, "You have no manners! How many times have I told you but you never understand? How much longer do you want me to keep telling you?" Allow him to retain his honour and make him feel that he is valued, even if he makes mistakes. Do you know why? Because the objective here is to correct the error, and not to take revenge or dishonour him. Meaning, dear reader, to say it plainly, no one likes to be ordered around. Look at the Prophet's approach in this regard. One day he decided to advise 'Abdullah bin 'Umar to pray the night prayers. Make your tongue sweet He did not call him and say, "O 'Abdullah, pray the night prayer!" Rather, he advised him as if he was making a suggestion saying, "How nice is 'Abdullah! If only he were to pray the night prayers also!" In another narration he said, "O 'Abdullah, do not be like so-and-so. He used to pray at night, but then he left it." In fact, if you are able to bring his mistake to his attention without him realising, then this is the best approach. A man sneezed in the company of 'Abdullah bin al-Mubarak but did not say: 'al-Hamdulillah'. So 'Abdullah bin al-Mubarak said, "What does a person say when he sneezes?" He said, "al-Hamdulillah." 'Abdullah bin al-Mubarak said, "YarhamukAllah" (May Allah have mercy on you). The Prophet had a similar approach. When he would conclude his 'Asr prayer, he would visit his wives one-by-one to sit and talk with them. Once he visited Zaynab bint Jahsh and found honey with her. The Prophet used to love honey and other sweets. He began to drink honey and talk to her, and ended up staying with her for longer than he would stay with anyone else. Thereupon, 'A'ishah and Hafsah became jealous and planned that whoever of the two the Prophet visits should say, "I ﬁnd that you smell of Maghaﬁr!" Maghaﬁr is a sweet drink, similar to honey but has an unpleasant smell. The Prophet was always concerned that his body or mouth did not smell bad, as he used to speak with Jibril and the people. When he visited Hafsah she asked him what he had eaten. He said, "I had some honey to drink when I visited Zaynab." Make your tongue sweet She said, "I ﬁnd the smell of Maghaﬁr on you!" He said, "No. I only had some honey to drink. But I won't drink it, again." He then went to 'A'ishah and she said the same to him. Days went by and Allah ﬁnally revealed the secret to the Prophet . After a few days, the Prophet informed Hafsah of a matter in conﬁdence but she betrayed the conﬁdentiality.He visited her one day whilst she had a guest known as ash-Shifa' bint 'Abdullah. She was a female Companion and a medical student who used to treat patients. The Prophet wanted to bring her attention to her mistake by approaching the issue indirectly, in order to be gentle with her.
content_id: Artinya, jika nasihat diberikan dengan cara yang tepat dan penuh kelembutan sejak awal, hasilnya pun akan serupa. Namun jika disampaikan dengan kasar dan tanpa perasaan, hasilnya pun akan sama. Ketika kita menasihati orang lain, kita sebenarnya sedang berhadapan dengan hati mereka, bukan tubuh mereka. Itulah mengapa anak-anak sering menerima nasihat ibu tetapi tidak nasihat ayah, atau sebaliknya, dan murid-murid cenderung menerima nasihat satu guru tetapi bukan guru yang lain. Keterampilan pertama yang harus digunakan saat memberi nasihat adalah tidak berlebihan dan tidak mengungkit setiap kesalahan, besar maupun kecil, agar orang lain tidak merasa bahwa kamu selalu mengawasi setiap gerak-gerik mereka. Jika tidak, mereka akan melihatmu sebagai orang yang menyusahkan. Pemimpin suatu kaum bukanlah orang yang bodoh, pemimpin suatu kaum adalah orang yang berpura-pura bodoh. Jika kamu bisa menyampaikan nasihatmu sebagai saran, maka lakukanlah. Misalnya, istrimu membawakan makan malam dan kamu tahu ia telah bersusah payah memasaknya, namun ternyata masakannya terlalu asin. Jangan berkata, "Astaga! Masakan apa ini? Na'udzubillah! Sepertinya kamu menuangkan seluruh bungkus garam ke dalamnya!" Sebaliknya, katakanlah, "Kalau garamnya dikurangi sedikit, pasti rasanya lebih enak lagi." Demikian pula, jika kamu melihat anakmu mengenakan pakaian kotor, nasihati dia seolah memberi saran, karena orang tidak suka diperintah-perintah. Katakan padanya, "Alangkah baiknya jika kamu mengenakan pakaian yang lebih bersih." Jika seorang murid datang terlambat ke sekolah, katakan padanya, "Alangkah baiknya jika kamu tidak terlambat lagi." Pendekatan ini jauh lebih baik. Gunakan pendekatan ini setiap saat: "Bagaimana kalau kamu melakukan ini? Aku sarankan kamu melakukan itu..." Pendekatan ini jauh lebih baik daripada kamu berkata, "Kamu tidak punya adab! Sudah berapa kali aku katakan tapi kamu tidak pernah mengerti? Sampai kapan kamu mau aku terus-terusan mengatakan ini?" Biarkan ia mempertahankan kehormatannya dan buat ia merasa dihargai, meskipun ia membuat kesalahan. Tahukah kamu mengapa? Karena tujuannya di sini adalah memperbaiki kesalahan, bukan membalas dendam atau mempermalukan dia. Secara singkat, pembaca yang budiman, tidak ada orang yang suka diperintah-perintah. Lihatlah pendekatan Nabi dalam hal ini. Suatu hari beliau ingin menasihati Abdullah bin Umar agar shalat malam. Beliau tidak memanggilnya dan berkata, "Wahai Abdullah, shalatlah malam!" Sebaliknya, beliau menasihatinya seolah memberi saran dengan berkata, "Alangkah baiknya Abdullah! Andai saja ia juga shalat malam!" Dalam riwayat lain beliau bersabda, "Wahai Abdullah, janganlah kamu seperti si fulan. Ia biasa shalat malam, tapi kemudian meninggalkannya." Bahkan, jika kamu bisa menunjukkan kesalahannya tanpa ia menyadari, maka itulah pendekatan terbaik. Seseorang bersin di hadapan Abdullah bin al-Mubarak namun tidak mengucapkan "al-Hamdulillah". Maka Abdullah bin al-Mubarak berkata, "Apa yang diucapkan seseorang ketika bersin?" Orang itu berkata, "al-Hamdulillah." Abdullah bin al-Mubarak pun berkata, "YarhamukAllah" (Semoga Allah merahmatimu). Nabi memiliki pendekatan yang serupa. Setelah shalat Ashar, beliau biasa mengunjungi istri-istrinya satu per satu untuk duduk dan berbincang bersama mereka. Suatu kali beliau mengunjungi Zaynab binti Jahsy dan menemukan madu di sana. Nabi sangat menyukai madu dan makanan manis lainnya. Beliau mulai minum madu dan berbincang dengannya, dan akhirnya tinggal lebih lama dari yang biasanya beliau lakukan bersama siapapun. Maka Aisyah dan Hafshah pun merasa cemburu dan merencanakannya berdua bahwa siapapun dari keduanya yang dikunjungi Nabi harus mengatakan, "Aku mencium bau mughafir darimu!" Mughafir adalah minuman manis yang mirip madu namun berbau tidak sedap. Nabi selalu peduli agar tubuh atau mulutnya tidak berbau tidak enak, karena beliau biasa berbicara dengan Jibril dan dengan orang-orang. Ketika beliau mengunjungi Hafshah, ia bertanya apa yang telah beliau makan. Beliau berkata, "Aku minum madu ketika mengunjungi Zaynab." Ia berkata, "Aku mencium bau mughafir darimu!" Beliau berkata, "Tidak. Aku hanya minum madu. Tapi aku tidak akan meminumnya lagi." Kemudian beliau pergi ke Aisyah dan ia pun mengatakan hal yang sama. Hari-hari berlalu dan akhirnya Allah membukakan rahasianya kepada Nabi. Beberapa hari kemudian, Nabi memberitahu Hafshah suatu hal secara rahasia namun ia membocorkannya. Suatu hari beliau mengunjunginya saat ia sedang menerima tamu yang dikenal sebagai asy-Syifa' binti Abdullah. Ia adalah seorang shahabiyah dan pelajar kedokteran yang biasa mengobati pasien. Nabi ingin menunjukkan kesalahan Hafshah dengan mendekati masalah itu secara tidak langsung agar bersikap lembut terhadapnya.
